:LABEL: CWE
name:string: J2EE Misconfiguration: Data Transmission Without Encryption
description:string: Information sent over a network can be compromised while in transit. An attacker may be able to read or modify the contents if the data are sent in plaintext or are weakly encrypted.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Creating Debug Binary
description:string: Debugging messages help attackers learn about the system and plan a form of attack.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Password in Configuration File
description:string: Storing a plaintext password in a configuration file allows anyone who can read the file access to the password-protected resource making them an easy target for attackers.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: External Control of System or Configuration Setting
description:string: One or more system settings or configuration elements can be externally controlled by a user.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Input Validation
description:string: The product receives input or data, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the properties that are required to process the data safely and correctly.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
description:string: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the product does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location that is outside of the restricted directory.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Relative Path Traversal
description:string: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize sequences such as .. that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Absolute Path Traversal
description:string: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize absolute path sequences such as /abs/path that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
description:string: The product attempts to access a file based on the filename, but it does not properly prevent that filename from identifying a link or shortcut that resolves to an unintended resource.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following
description:string: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the file is a symbolic link that resolves to a target outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Windows Hard Link
description:string: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the name is associated with a hard link to a target that is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: External Control of File Name or Path
description:string: The product allows user input to control or influence paths or file names that are used in filesystem operations.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of a command, data structure, or record using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify how it is parsed or interpreted when it is sent to a downstream component.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements
description:string: The product correctly neutralizes certain special elements, but it improperly neutralizes equivalent special elements.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of a command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended command when it is sent to a downstream component.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended OS command when it is sent to a downstream component.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
description:string: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags in a Web Page (Basic XSS)
description:string: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters such as <, >, and & that could be interpreted as web-scripting elements when they are sent to a downstream component that processes web pages.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes in a Web Page
description:string: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes javascript: or other URIs from dangerous attributes within tags, such as onmouseover, onload, onerror, or style.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Invalid Characters in Identifiers in Web Pages
description:string: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes invalid characters or byte sequences in the middle of tag names, URI schemes, and other identifiers.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
description:string: The product constructs a string for a command to be executed by a separate component in another control sphere, but it does not properly delimit the intended arguments, options, or switches within that command string.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command when it is sent to a downstream component. Without sufficient removal or quoting of SQL syntax in user-controllable inputs, the generated SQL query can cause those inputs to be interpreted as SQL instead of ordinary user data.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of an LDAP query using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended LDAP query when it is sent to a downstream component.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)
description:string: The product does not properly neutralize special elements that are used in XML, allowing attackers to modify the syntax, content, or commands of the XML before it is processed by an end system.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')
description:string: The product uses CRLF (carriage return line feeds) as a special element, e.g. to separate lines or records, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CRLF sequences from inputs.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the syntax or behavior of the intended code segment.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')
description:string: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before using the input in a dynamic evaluation call (e.g. eval).

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
description:string: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before inserting the input into an executable resource, such as a library, configuration file, or template.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Server-Side Includes (SSI) Within a Web Page
description:string: The product generates a web page, but does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input that could be interpreted as a server-side include (SSI) directive.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')
description:string: The PHP application receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before its usage in require, include, or similar functions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
description:string: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before it is used as an identifier for a resource that may be outside the intended sphere of control.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Struts: Incomplete validate() Method Definition
description:string: The product has a validator form that either does not define a validate() method, or defines a validate() method but does not call super.validate().

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Struts: Form Bean Does Not Extend Validation Class
description:string: If a form bean does not extend an ActionForm subclass of the Validator framework, it can expose the application to other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing XML Validation
description:string: The product accepts XML from an untrusted source but does not validate the XML against the proper schema.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')
description:string: The product receives data from an HTTP agent/component (e.g., web server, proxy, browser, etc.), but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CR and LF characters before the data is included in outgoing HTTP headers.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Process Control
description:string: Executing commands or loading libraries from an untrusted source or in an untrusted environment can cause an application to execute malicious commands (and payloads) on behalf of an attacker.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Misinterpretation of Input
description:string: The product misinterprets an input, whether from an attacker or another product, in a security-relevant fashion.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
description:string: The product prepares a structured message for communication with another component, but encoding or escaping of the data is either missing or done incorrectly. As a result, the intended structure of the message is not preserved.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
description:string: The product constructs a log message from external input, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements when the message is written to a log file.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Validation of Array Index
description:string: The product uses untrusted input when calculating or using an array index, but the product does not validate or incorrectly validates the index to ensure the index references a valid position within the array.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Handling of Invalid Use of Special Elements
description:string: The product does not properly filter, remove, quote, or otherwise manage the invalid use of special elements in user-controlled input, which could cause adverse effect on its behavior and integrity.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Permissive List of Allowed Inputs
description:string: The product implements a protection mechanism that relies on a list of inputs (or properties of inputs) that are explicitly allowed by policy because the inputs are assumed to be safe, but the list is too permissive - that is, it allows an input that is unsafe, leading to resultant weaknesses.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description:string: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Sent Data
description:string: The code transmits data to another actor, but a portion of the data includes sensitive information that should not be accessible to that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
description:string: The product generates an error message that includes sensitive information about its environment, users, or associated data.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code
description:string: The product inserts sensitive information into debugging code, which could expose this information if the debugging code is not disabled in production.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Storage of File with Sensitive Data Under Web Root
description:string: The product stores sensitive data under the web document root with insufficient access control, which might make it accessible to untrusted parties.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Information Loss or Omission
description:string: The product does not record, or improperly records, security-relevant information that leads to an incorrect decision or hampers later analysis.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Omission of Security-relevant Information
description:string: The product does not record or display information that would be important for identifying the source or nature of an attack, or determining if an action is safe.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Failure to Handle Missing Parameter
description:string: If too few arguments are sent to a function, the function will still pop the expected number of arguments from the stack. Potentially, a variable number of arguments could be exhausted in a function as well.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Handling of Extra Parameters
description:string: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when the number of parameters, fields, or arguments with the same name exceeds the expected amount.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Uncaught Exception
description:string: An exception is thrown from a function, but it is not caught.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unchecked Return Value
description:string: The product does not check the return value from a method or function, which can prevent it from detecting unexpected states and conditions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Plaintext Storage of a Password
description:string: The product stores a password in plaintext within resources such as memory or files.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Empty Password in Configuration File
description:string: Using an empty string as a password is insecure.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Hard-coded Password
description:string: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Password in Configuration File
description:string: The product stores a password in a configuration file that might be accessible to actors who do not know the password.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Weak Encoding for Password
description:string: Obscuring a password with a trivial encoding does not protect the password.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Privilege Assignment
description:string: A product incorrectly assigns a privilege to a particular actor, creating an unintended sphere of control for that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Privilege Management
description:string: The product does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor, creating an unintended sphere of control for that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Handling of Insufficient Privileges
description:string: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when it has insufficient privileges to perform an operation, leading to resultant weaknesses.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Default Permissions
description:string: During installation, installed file permissions are set to allow anyone to modify those files.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Handling of Insufficient Permissions or Privileges
description:string: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when it has insufficient privileges to access resources or functionality as specified by their permissions. This may cause it to follow unexpected code paths that may leave the product in an invalid state.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Preservation of Permissions
description:string: The product does not preserve permissions or incorrectly preserves permissions when copying, restoring, or sharing objects, which can cause them to have less restrictive permissions than intended.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Ownership Management
description:string: The product assigns the wrong ownership, or does not properly verify the ownership, of an object or resource.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unverified Ownership
description:string: The product does not properly verify that a critical resource is owned by the proper entity.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Access Control
description:string: The product does not restrict or incorrectly restricts access to a resource from an unauthorized actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Authorization
description:string: The product does not perform or incorrectly performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect User Management
description:string: The product does not properly manage a user within its environment.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Authentication
description:string: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the product does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel
description:string: The product requires authentication, but the product has an alternate path or channel that does not require authentication.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass by Alternate Name
description:string: The product performs authentication based on the name of a resource being accessed, or the name of the actor performing the access, but it does not properly check all possible names for that resource or actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass by Spoofing
description:string: This attack-focused weakness is caused by incorrectly implemented authentication schemes that are subject to spoofing attacks.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on IP Address for Authentication
description:string: The product uses an IP address for authentication.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Using Referer Field for Authentication
description:string: The referer field in HTTP requests can be easily modified and, as such, is not a valid means of message integrity checking.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass by Capture-replay
description:string: A capture-replay flaw exists when the design of the product makes it possible for a malicious user to sniff network traffic and bypass authentication by replaying it to the server in question to the same effect as the original message (or with minor changes).

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Certificate Validation
description:string: The product does not validate, or incorrectly validates, a certificate.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Following of a Certificate's Chain of Trust
description:string: The product does not follow, or incorrectly follows, the chain of trust for a certificate back to a trusted root certificate.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Validation of Certificate with Host Mismatch
description:string: The product communicates with a host that provides a certificate, but the product does not properly ensure that the certificate is actually associated with that host.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Validation of Certificate Expiration
description:string: A certificate expiration is not validated or is incorrectly validated.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Check for Certificate Revocation
description:string: The product does not check or incorrectly checks the revocation status of a certificate, which may cause it to use a certificate that has been compromised.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Channel Accessible by Non-Endpoint
description:string: The product does not adequately verify the identity of actors at both ends of a communication channel, or does not adequately ensure the integrity of the channel, in a way that allows the channel to be accessed or influenced by an actor that is not an endpoint.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass by Assumed-Immutable Data
description:string: The authentication scheme or implementation uses key data elements that are assumed to be immutable, but can be controlled or modified by the attacker.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Implementation of Authentication Algorithm
description:string: The requirements for the product dictate the use of an established authentication algorithm, but the implementation of the algorithm is incorrect.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Critical Step in Authentication
description:string: The product implements an authentication technique, but it skips a step that weakens the technique.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authentication Bypass by Primary Weakness
description:string: The authentication algorithm is sound, but the implemented mechanism can be bypassed as the result of a separate weakness that is primary to the authentication error.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
description:string: The product does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes a significant amount of resources.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
description:string: The product does not implement sufficient measures to prevent multiple failed authentication attempts within a short time frame.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Single-factor Authentication
description:string: The product uses an authentication algorithm that uses a single factor (e.g., a password) in a security context that should require more than one factor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Password System for Primary Authentication
description:string: The use of password systems as the primary means of authentication may be subject to several flaws or shortcomings, each reducing the effectiveness of the mechanism.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
description:string: The product does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
description:string: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Storage in a File or on Disk
description:string: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a file, or on disk.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in a Cookie
description:string: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a cookie.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory
description:string: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in memory.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
description:string: The product transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext in a communication channel that can be sniffed by unauthorized actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
description:string: The product uses a hard-coded, unchangeable cryptographic key.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Key Exchange without Entity Authentication
description:string: The product performs a key exchange with an actor without verifying the identity of that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reusing a Nonce, Key Pair in Encryption
description:string: Nonces should be used for the present occasion and only once.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of a Key Past its Expiration Date
description:string: The product uses a cryptographic key or password past its expiration date, which diminishes its safety significantly by increasing the timing window for cracking attacks against that key.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Cryptographic Step
description:string: The product does not implement a required step in a cryptographic algorithm, resulting in weaker encryption than advertised by the algorithm.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inadequate Encryption Strength
description:string: The product stores or transmits sensitive data using an encryption scheme that is theoretically sound, but is not strong enough for the level of protection required.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
description:string: The product uses a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Weak Hash
description:string: The product uses an algorithm that produces a digest (output value) that does not meet security expectations for a hash function that allows an adversary to reasonably determine the original input (preimage attack), find another input that can produce the same hash (2nd preimage attack), or find multiple inputs that evaluate to the same hash (birthday attack).

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Generation of Predictable IV with CBC Mode
description:string: The product generates and uses a predictable initialization Vector (IV) with Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) Mode, which causes algorithms to be susceptible to dictionary attacks when they are encrypted under the same key.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
description:string: The product uses insufficiently random numbers or values in a security context that depends on unpredictable numbers.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficient Entropy
description:string: The product uses an algorithm or scheme that produces insufficient entropy, leaving patterns or clusters of values that are more likely to occur than others.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficient Entropy in PRNG
description:string: The lack of entropy available for, or used by, a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) can be a stability and security threat.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Small Space of Random Values
description:string: The number of possible random values is smaller than needed by the product, making it more susceptible to brute force attacks.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
description:string: The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) but does not correctly manage seeds.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Same Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
description:string: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses the same seed each time the product is initialized.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
description:string: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) is initialized from a predictable seed, such as the process ID or system time.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
description:string: The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) in a security context, but the PRNG's algorithm is not cryptographically strong.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers
description:string: The product uses a scheme that generates numbers or identifiers that are more predictable than required.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Predictable Exact Value from Previous Values
description:string: An exact value or random number can be precisely predicted by observing previous values.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
description:string: The product does not sufficiently verify the origin or authenticity of data, in a way that causes it to accept invalid data.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Origin Validation Error
description:string: The product does not properly verify that the source of data or communication is valid.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
description:string: The product does not verify, or incorrectly verifies, the cryptographic signature for data.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Reverse DNS Resolution for a Security-Critical Action
description:string: The product performs reverse DNS resolution on an IP address to obtain the hostname and make a security decision, but it does not properly ensure that the IP address is truly associated with the hostname.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description:string: The web application does not, or cannot, sufficiently verify whether a request was intentionally provided by the user who sent the request, which could have originated from an unauthorized actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Support for Integrity Check
description:string: The product uses a transmission protocol that does not include a mechanism for verifying the integrity of the data during transmission, such as a checksum.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor
description:string: The product does not properly prevent a person's private, personal information from being accessed by actors who either (1) are not explicitly authorized to access the information or (2) do not have the implicit consent of the person about whom the information is collected.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
description:string: The product contains a concurrent code sequence that requires temporary, exclusive access to a shared resource, but a timing window exists in which the shared resource can be modified by another code sequence operating concurrently.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Divide By Zero
description:string: The product divides a value by zero.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insecure Temporary File
description:string: Creating and using insecure temporary files can leave application and system data vulnerable to attack.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Creation of Temporary File in Directory with Insecure Permissions
description:string: The product creates a temporary file in a directory whose permissions allow unintended actors to determine the file's existence or otherwise access that file.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: J2EE Bad Practices: Use of System.exit()
description:string: A J2EE application uses System.exit(), which also shuts down its container.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Session Fixation
description:string: Authenticating a user, or otherwise establishing a new user session, without invalidating any existing session identifier gives an attacker the opportunity to steal authenticated sessions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Detection of Error Condition Without Action
description:string: The product detects a specific error, but takes no actions to handle the error.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unchecked Error Condition
description:string: [PLANNED FOR DEPRECATION. SEE MAINTENANCE NOTES AND CONSIDER CWE-252, CWE-248, OR CWE-1069.] Ignoring exceptions and other error conditions may allow an attacker to induce unexpected behavior unnoticed.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
description:string: The product does not properly check when a function or operation returns a value that is legitimate for the function, but is not expected by the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Declaration of Catch for Generic Exception
description:string: Catching overly broad exceptions promotes complex error handling code that is more likely to contain security vulnerabilities.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Declaration of Throws for Generic Exception
description:string: The product throws or raises an overly broad exceptions that can hide important details and produce inappropriate responses to certain conditions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Transmission of Private Resources into a New Sphere ('Resource Leak')
description:string: The product makes resources available to untrusted parties when those resources are only intended to be accessed by the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unprotected Primary Channel
description:string: The product uses a primary channel for administration or restricted functionality, but it does not properly protect the channel.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Protection of Alternate Path
description:string: The product does not sufficiently protect all possible paths that a user can take to access restricted functionality or resources.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Direct Request ('Forced Browsing')
description:string: The web application does not adequately enforce appropriate authorization on all restricted URLs, scripts, or files.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Untrusted Search Path
description:string: The product searches for critical resources using an externally-supplied search path that can point to resources that are not under the product's direct control.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Uncontrolled Search Path Element
description:string: The product uses a fixed or controlled search path to find resources, but one or more locations in that path can be under the control of unintended actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
description:string: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Interpretation Conflict
description:string: Product A handles inputs or steps differently than Product B, which causes A to perform incorrect actions based on its perception of B's state.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unintended Proxy or Intermediary ('Confused Deputy')
description:string: The product receives a request, message, or directive from an upstream component, but the product does not sufficiently preserve the original source of the request before forwarding the request to an external actor that is outside of the product's control sphere. This causes the product to appear to be the source of the request, leading it to act as a proxy or other intermediary between the upstream component and the external actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')
description:string: The product acts as an intermediary HTTP agent (such as a proxy or firewall) in the data flow between two entities such as a client and server, but it does not interpret malformed HTTP requests or responses in ways that are consistent with how the messages will be processed by those entities that are at the ultimate destination.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unimplemented or Unsupported Feature in UI
description:string: A UI function for a security feature appears to be supported and gives feedback to the user that suggests that it is supported, but the underlying functionality is not implemented.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information
description:string: The user interface (UI) does not properly represent critical information to the user, allowing the information - or its source - to be obscured or spoofed. This is often a component in phishing attacks.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: External Initialization of Trusted Variables or Data Stores
description:string: The product initializes critical internal variables or data stores using inputs that can be modified by untrusted actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception
description:string: The product does not clean up its state or incorrectly cleans up its state when an exception is thrown, leading to unexpected state or control flow.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection')
description:string: The product uses external input with reflection to select which classes or code to use, but it does not sufficiently prevent the input from selecting improper classes or code.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: External Control of Assumed-Immutable Web Parameter
description:string: The web application does not sufficiently verify inputs that are assumed to be immutable but are actually externally controllable, such as hidden form fields.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: NULL Pointer Dereference
description:string: The product dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid but is NULL.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Obsolete Function
description:string: The code uses deprecated or obsolete functions, which suggests that the code has not been actively reviewed or maintained.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Default Case in Multiple Condition Expression
description:string: The code does not have a default case in an expression with multiple conditions, such as a switch statement.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Omitted Break Statement in Switch
description:string: The product omits a break statement within a switch or similar construct, causing code associated with multiple conditions to execute. This can cause problems when the programmer only intended to execute code associated with one condition.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Active Debug Code
description:string: The product is released with debugging code still enabled or active.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Critical Public Variable Without Final Modifier
description:string: The product has a critical public variable that is not final, which allows the variable to be modified to contain unexpected values.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
description:string: The product downloads source code or an executable from a remote location and executes the code without sufficiently verifying the origin and integrity of the code.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
description:string: The product does not properly prevent sensitive system-level information from being accessed by unauthorized actors who do not have the same level of access to the underlying system as the product does.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Public Static Field Not Marked Final
description:string: An object contains a public static field that is not marked final, which might allow it to be modified in unexpected ways.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Trust Boundary Violation
description:string: The product mixes trusted and untrusted data in the same data structure or structured message.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description:string: The product deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently ensuring that the resulting data will be valid.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Embedded Malicious Code
description:string: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Replicating Malicious Code (Virus or Worm)
description:string: Replicating malicious code, including viruses and worms, will attempt to attack other systems once it has successfully compromised the target system or the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Weak Password Requirements
description:string: The product does not require that users should have strong passwords.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
description:string: The product transmits or stores authentication credentials, but it uses an insecure method that is susceptible to unauthorized interception and/or retrieval.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unprotected Transport of Credentials
description:string: Login pages do not use adequate measures to protect the user name and password while they are in transit from the client to the server.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information
description:string: The web application does not use an appropriate caching policy that specifies the extent to which each web page and associated form fields should be cached.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in an Environment Variable
description:string: The product uses an environment variable to store unencrypted sensitive information.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
description:string: The product writes sensitive information to a log file.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory
description:string: The product places sensitive information into files or directories that are accessible to actors who are allowed to have access to the files, but not to the sensitive information.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Persistent Cookies Containing Sensitive Information
description:string: The web application uses persistent cookies, but the cookies contain sensitive information.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code
description:string: Source code on a web server or repository often contains sensitive information and should generally not be accessible to users.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Hard-coded, Security-relevant Constants
description:string: The product uses hard-coded constants instead of symbolic names for security-critical values, which increases the likelihood of mistakes during code maintenance or security policy change.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing
description:string: The product inappropriately exposes a directory listing with an index of all the resources located inside of the directory.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
description:string: Certain conditions, such as network failure, will cause a server error message to be displayed.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties
description:string: The product makes files or directories accessible to unauthorized actors, even though they should not be.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: SQL Injection: Hibernate
description:string: Using Hibernate to execute a dynamic SQL statement built with user-controlled input can allow an attacker to modify the statement's meaning or to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking
description:string: The product relies on the existence or values of cookies when performing security-critical operations, but it does not properly ensure that the setting is valid for the associated user.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled SQL Primary Key
description:string: The product uses a database table that includes records that should not be accessible to an actor, but it executes a SQL statement with a primary key that can be controlled by that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of HTTP Request With Sensitive Query String
description:string: The web application uses an HTTP method to process a request, but the request includes sensitive information in the query string.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
description:string: The web application accepts a user-controlled input that specifies a link to an external site, and uses that link in a redirect.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Client-Side Enforcement of Server-Side Security
description:string: The product is composed of a server that relies on the client to implement a mechanism that is intended to protect the server.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere
description:string: The product uses an externally controlled name or reference that resolves to a resource that is outside of the intended control sphere.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
description:string: The product processes an XML document that can contain XML entities with URIs that resolve to documents outside of the intended sphere of control, causing the product to embed incorrect documents into its output.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficient Session Expiration
description:string: According to WASC, Insufficient Session Expiration is when a web site permits an attacker to reuse old session credentials or session IDs for authorization.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute
description:string: The Secure attribute for sensitive cookies in HTTPS sessions is not set.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code Comments
description:string: While adding general comments is very useful, some programmers tend to leave important data, such as: filenames related to the web application, old links or links which were not meant to be browsed by users, old code fragments, etc.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Unverified Password Change
description:string: When setting a new password for a user, the product does not require knowledge of the original password, or using another form of authentication.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Function Call with Incorrectly Specified Arguments
description:string: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine with arguments that are not correctly specified, leading to always-incorrect behavior and resultant weaknesses.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open')
description:string: When the product encounters an error condition or failure, its design requires it to fall back to a state that is less secure than other options that are available, such as selecting the weakest encryption algorithm or using the most permissive access control restrictions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description:string: The system's authorization functionality does not prevent one user from gaining access to another user's data or record by modifying the key value identifying the data.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
description:string: The product contains a mechanism for users to recover or change their passwords without knowing the original password, but the mechanism is weak.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: External Control of Critical State Data
description:string: The product stores security-critical state information about its users, or the product itself, in a location that is accessible to unauthorized actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
description:string: The product uses external input to dynamically construct an XPath expression used to retrieve data from an XML database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input. This allows an attacker to control the structure of the query.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
description:string: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes web scripting syntax in HTTP headers that can be used by web browser components that can process raw headers, such as Flash.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on File Name or Extension of Externally-Supplied File
description:string: The product allows a file to be uploaded, but it relies on the file name or extension of the file to determine the appropriate behaviors. This could be used by attackers to cause the file to be misclassified and processed in a dangerous fashion.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization
description:string: The product does not properly compartmentalize or isolate functionality, processes, or resources that require different privilege levels, rights, or permissions.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Security Through Obscurity
description:string: The product uses a protection mechanism whose strength depends heavily on its obscurity, such that knowledge of its algorithms or key data is sufficient to defeat the mechanism.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Violation of Secure Design Principles
description:string: The product violates well-established principles for secure design.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
description:string: The product exposes a resource to the wrong control sphere, providing unintended actors with inappropriate access to the resource.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function
description:string: The product invokes a potentially dangerous function that could introduce a vulnerability if it is used incorrectly, but the function can also be used safely.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Protection Mechanism Failure
description:string: The product does not use or incorrectly uses a protection mechanism that provides sufficient defense against directed attacks against the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
description:string: The product does not properly anticipate or handle exceptional conditions that rarely occur during normal operation of the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
description:string: The product specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Exposed Dangerous Method or Function
description:string: The product provides an Applications Programming Interface (API) or similar interface for interaction with external actors, but the interface includes a dangerous method or function that is not properly restricted.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
description:string: The product does not check or incorrectly checks for unusual or exceptional conditions that are not expected to occur frequently during day to day operation of the product.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
description:string: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles an exceptional condition.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Custom Error Page
description:string: The product does not return custom error pages to the user, possibly exposing sensitive information.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')
description:string: A protocol or its implementation supports interaction between multiple actors and allows those actors to negotiate which algorithm should be used as a protection mechanism such as encryption or authentication, but it does not select the strongest algorithm that is available to both parties.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of a One-Way Hash without a Salt
description:string: The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product does not also use a salt as part of the input.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of a One-Way Hash with a Predictable Salt
description:string: The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product uses a predictable salt as part of the input.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')
description:string: The product uses XML documents and allows their structure to be defined with a Document Type Definition (DTD), but it does not properly control the number of recursive definitions of entities.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insufficient Logging
description:string: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of RSA Algorithm without OAEP
description:string: The product uses the RSA algorithm but does not incorporate Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding (OAEP), which might weaken the encryption.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking in a Security Decision
description:string: The product uses a protection mechanism that relies on the existence or values of a cookie, but it does not properly ensure that the cookie is valid for the associated user.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
description:string: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Control of Interaction Frequency
description:string: The product does not properly limit the number or frequency of interactions that it has with an actor, such as the number of incoming requests.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
description:string: The product uses a protection mechanism that relies on the existence or values of an input, but the input can be modified by an untrusted actor in a way that bypasses the protection mechanism.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
description:string: The product imports, requires, or includes executable functionality (such as a library) from a source that is outside of the intended control sphere.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source
description:string: The product includes web functionality (such as a web widget) from another domain, which causes it to operate within the domain of the product, potentially granting total access and control of the product to the untrusted source.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow
description:string: The product supports a session in which more than one behavior must be performed by an actor, but it does not properly ensure that the actor performs the behaviors in the required sequence.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Missing Authorization
description:string: The product does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrect Authorization
description:string: The product performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action, but it does not correctly perform the check.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
description:string: The product receives input from an upstream component that specifies multiple attributes, properties, or fields that are to be initialized or updated in an object, but it does not properly control which attributes can be modified.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort
description:string: The product generates a hash for a password, but it uses a scheme that does not provide a sufficient level of computational effort that would make password cracking attacks infeasible or expensive.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an Expression Language Statement ('Expression Language Injection')
description:string: The product constructs all or part of an expression language (EL) statement in a framework such as a Java Server Page (JSP) using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended EL statement before it is executed.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description:string: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information
description:string: The product stores sensitive information without properly limiting read or write access by unauthorized actors.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Export of Android Application Components
description:string: The Android application exports a component for use by other applications, but does not properly restrict which applications can launch the component or access the data it contains.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel
description:string: The product establishes a communication channel to handle an incoming request that has been initiated by an actor, but it does not properly verify that the request is coming from the expected origin.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Incorrectly Specified Destination in a Communication Channel
description:string: The product creates a communication channel to initiate an outgoing request to an actor, but it does not correctly specify the intended destination for that actor.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Permissive Cross-domain Security Policy with Untrusted Domains
description:string: The product uses a web-client protection mechanism such as a Content Security Policy (CSP) or cross-domain policy file, but the policy includes untrusted domains with which the web client is allowed to communicate.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
description:string: The product uses a cookie to store sensitive information, but the cookie is not marked with the HttpOnly flag.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
description:string: The web application does not restrict or incorrectly restricts frame objects or UI layers that belong to another application or domain.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Web Link to Untrusted Target with window.opener Access
description:string: The web application produces links to untrusted external sites outside of its sphere of control, but it does not properly prevent the external site from modifying security-critical properties of the window.opener object, such as the location property.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Unmaintained Third Party Components
description:string: The product relies on third-party components that are not actively supported or maintained by the original developer or a trusted proxy for the original developer.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Excessive Attack Surface
description:string: The product has an attack surface whose quantitative measurement exceeds a desirable maximum.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Improper Model Validation
description:string: The ASP.NET application does not use, or incorrectly uses, the model validation framework.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of a Cryptographic Primitive with a Risky Implementation
description:string: To fulfill the need for a cryptographic primitive, the product implements a cryptographic algorithm using a non-standard, unproven, or disallowed/non-compliant cryptographic implementation.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Predictable Algorithm in Random Number Generator
description:string: The device uses an algorithm that is predictable and generates a pseudo-random number.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute
description:string: The SameSite attribute for sensitive cookies is not set, or an insecure value is used.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Reliance on Component That is Not Updateable
description:string: The product contains a component that cannot be updated or patched in order to remove vulnerabilities or significant bugs.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Weak Authentication
description:string: The product uses an authentication mechanism to restrict access to specific users or identities, but the mechanism does not sufficiently prove that the claimed identity is correct.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Weak Credentials
description:string: The product uses weak credentials (such as a default key or hard-coded password) that can be calculated, derived, reused, or guessed by an attacker.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Default Credentials
description:string: The product uses default credentials (such as passwords or cryptographic keys) for potentially critical functionality.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Use of Default Password
description:string: The product uses default passwords for potentially critical functionality.

:LABEL: CWE
name:string: Dependency on Vulnerable Third-Party Component
description:string: The product has a dependency on a third-party component that contains one or more known vulnerabilities.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A01:2025 - Broken Access Control
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A01 category "Broken Access Control" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A02 category "Security Misconfiguration" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A03:2025 - Software Supply Chain Failures
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A03 category "Software Supply Chain Failures" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A04:2025 - Cryptographic Failures
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A04 category "Cryptographic Failures" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A05:2025 - Injection
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A05 category "Injection" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A06:2025 - Insecure Design
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A06 category "Insecure Design" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A07:2025 - Authentication Failures
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A07 category "Authentication Failures" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A08 category "Software or Data Integrity Failures" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A09:2025 - Logging and Alerting Failures
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A09 category "Logging and Alerting Failures" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_CATEGORY
name:string: OWASP Top Ten 2025 Category A10:2025 - Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the A10 category "Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions" in the OWASP Top Ten 2025.

:LABEL: OWASP_VIEW
name:string: Weaknesses in OWASP Top Ten RC1 (2025)
description:string: Weaknesses in this category are related to the OWASP Top Ten (2025). This category is provided to give overview of the OWASP Top Ten 2025.
